vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:claude-opus-4.8
vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF: daytime red + two-line layout for the rain warning (issue #26 remainder)

plan.title: Rain warning daytime red + two lines

narratives.Overview: Completes issue #26 on top of the 50%-larger rain line already in PR #31: in the Day theme the rain warning is colored red (instead of the blue accent) for contrast against the bright background, and the cue is split across two lines.
narratives.Goals:
1. Day theme: rain warning text is red, not blue.
2. The cue reads on two lines: 'Rain possible' then 'within ~Xhrs'.
3. Night/Amber styling is unchanged (their palettes are already red/amber).
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: In the Day theme the rain warning MUST render red; in Night/Amber it MUST keep the existing accent color.
FR-2: The rain cue MUST render on two lines: line 1 the 'Rain possible' message (with the rain icon), line 2 the 'within ~Xhrs' timeframe.
FR-3: The rain line MUST stay beneath the temperature row.
narratives.Architecture: Add ThemePalette.isDayPreset to detect the Day palette. Split WeatherSnapshot.rainText into rainPrimaryText ('Rain possible') and rainSecondaryText ('within ~Xhrs'); keep rainText for the combined accessibility label. WeatherBadgeView renders a two-line VStack and chooses a red color when palette.isDayPreset, otherwise palette.accentColor.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Unit-test rainPrimaryText/rainSecondaryText (including pluralization and nil when no rain) and ThemePalette.isDayPreset. Verify the badge visually on the simulator across themes.

narrative.Acceptance: Rain warning is red in Day, accent in Night/Amber.
'Rain possible' and 'within ~Xhrs' render on separate lines.
